subject: Ecology
title: What Is a Population?
description: A fast breakdown of What Is a Population? for builders.

hook.text: A population is a group of the same species living in the same area at the same time.

buildup.text: Ecologists count them, track birth and death rates, and map how they spread.

discovery.text: Population size depends on four forces: births, deaths, immigration, and emigration.

twist.text: Even small populations matter—if they carry unique genes, losing them is irreversible.

climax.text: Understanding populations helps predict outbreaks, extinctions, and recovery timelines.

punchline.text: Count the individuals. Predict the future.

quiz.question: What four forces determine population size?
